pi_items.source: Verified via Rails ActiveSupport::TimeZone where 'Berlin' maps to 'Europe/Berlin'. The verifier uses a substring check 'expected in actual', making 'Berlin' correct.
OpenProject source code maps Monday to '1' in UI settings.
Verified in OpenProject config/constants/settings/definition.rb allowed formats.
Verified in OpenProject config/constants/settings/definition.rb allowed formats.

privileged_info_summary: OpenProject's internal settings correctly map Monday to '1', DD.MM.YYYY to '%d.%m.%Y', and 24-hour time to '%H:%M'. The timezone 'Berlin' is represented internally as 'Europe/Berlin', which the verifier correctly matches via substring. Note that in OpenProject 15 (used in this environment), these settings have moved to 'Calendars and dates' > 'Date format' and 'Users and permissions' > 'User settings', and the underlying 'Setting.time_zone' was renamed to 'Setting.user_default_timezone', which causes the task's export script to crash with NoMethodError.
